message:
fix(channel): fail-fast on cancelled Cx and defer evidence recording until delivery succeeds

FaultSender::send now checks cx.checkpoint() before entering the fault
injection path and returns SendError::Cancelled immediately if the
context is already cancelled. Previously, a cancelled Cx would buffer
the message into the reorder buffer but then fail during auto-flush,
leaving the message silently stuck in the buffer with no way for the
caller to detect that delivery was not actually accepted.

Duplication evidence (record_duplication / messages_duplicated counter)
is now only recorded after the duplicate message is successfully
delivered to the inner channel. Previously it was recorded before
attempting delivery, inflating the duplication count when the receiver
was closed or the channel was full.

Reorder flush evidence (emit_fault_evidence "reorder_flush" and the
stat_reorder_flushes counter) is now deferred until the first message
in a flush batch is successfully delivered. Previously it was emitted
before any delivery attempt, producing flush evidence even when the
receiver was disconnected and no messages actually reached the consumer.

Tests added:
- cancelled_send_returns_error_without_buffering: verifies Cancelled
  is returned and buffer stays empty
- duplication_evidence_only_recorded_after_successful_delivery: verifies
  counter stays at 1 when dup delivery fails after receiver drop
- flush_evidence_only_recorded_after_first_delivery: verifies no flush
  evidence when receiver is disconnected before flush

Also adds src/channel/watch_leak_test.rs — a standalone test binary
that verifies watch channel receivers are properly dropped and wakers
are cleaned up when the channel is closed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
